title: Context and AI — Why It Matters

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Context. [pause] The single most important concept in practical AI use. [pause] And the most often misunderstood.

scenes.name: The problem
scenes.text: You ask AI a question. The answer is technically correct, completely useless. [pause] It didn't fail because it lacked knowledge. It failed because it lacked context.

scenes.name: Definition
scenes.text: <emphasis>Context</emphasis> is everything available to the AI when it generates a response — your question, your details, the conversation so far. [pause] Not the same as knowledge.

scenes.name: Staffroom
scenes.text: Imagine a colleague says: <emphasis>make me a worksheet</emphasis>. [pause] You would ask — what subject, what grade, how many questions. [pause] AI cannot ask those questions. It just guesses.

scenes.name: Quality
scenes.text: Context quality and response quality move together. [pause] <emphasis>Create a quiz about fractions</emphasis> — generic output. [pause] Specify Year 6, unlike denominators, 10 questions, answer key — dramatically more useful.

scenes.name: What helps
scenes.text: What context helps most in the classroom. [pause] Student age. Prior knowledge. Learning objectives. Output format. And — sometimes — the role the AI should adopt.

scenes.name: Mistakes
scenes.text: Two common mistakes. [pause] One — assuming AI will figure out what you mean. It won't. [pause] Two — overloading with irrelevant detail. More text doesn't equal better context. Relevance does.

scenes.name: Foundation
scenes.text: Context is the foundation for everything ahead — prompting, meta-prompting. [pause] And the good news? It is not a technical skill. It is something teachers already do every day.
